items.headline: Major commercial project planned at West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, representing significant investment in San José's commercial corridor. This is the only major commercial project in this permit batch and could bring new business activity to the area.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The City Council is promoting a business license amnesty program aimed at getting unlicensed businesses in shopping centers to comply with city regulations. This could affect local retail compliance and may benefit businesses that want to legalize their operations without penalties.

items.headline: City announces new economic development incentives for small businesses
items.summary: Cupertino is introducing incentive programs to support retail, small businesses, and startups in the area. This could make it easier and cheaper for entrepreneurs to open or expand businesses in Cupertino.

items.headline: City Presenting Community Development Block Grant Funding Opportunities
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission heard presentations from applicants seeking federal Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. CDBG funds support affordable housing, community services, and neighborhood development projects across Sunnyvale.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area plan to study land use and transportation changes
items.summary: The Planning Commission is examining the San Antonio Road area with a study session on land use and transportation alternatives. This could affect how the corridor develops and how residents travel through that part of Palo Alto.

items.headline: New retail vitality policies to be implemented through zoning code amendments
items.summary: The Commission is considering an ordinance to amend zoning and building regulations to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. This aims to support and strengthen retail districts across Palo Alto.

items.headline: New electrical transmission lines planned across wastewater facility
items.summary: LS Power Grid California will install electrical transmission infrastructure across the San José-Santa Clara Regional Wastewater Facility under a new easement agreement. This infrastructure project could affect local power distribution or grid capacity in the region.

items.headline: San José and Santa Clara pursuing direct potable reuse water facility
items.summary: The cities of San José and Santa Clara, along with Valley Water, are in exclusive negotiations to develop a Direct Potable Reuse facility on jointly-identified land. This facility would convert recycled water into drinking water, addressing the region's water supply challenges.
